payload.def: A mention of or direction to a source of information.
payload.ex: The footnote included a {word} to a study she hadn't read before.

payload.def: A list of sources used or referred to in a piece of writing.

payload.def: To express someone else's words or ideas using different words.

payload.def: A group of words taken exactly from a text or speech and repeated by someone else.

payload.def: A brief statement of the main points of something.

payload.def: A short summary of a longer academic or scientific paper.

payload.def: A short extract taken from a larger text.

payload.def: A short section of text from a longer piece of writing.

payload.def: Arranged in the order in which events occurred.

payload.def: Following a particular order, one after another.

payload.def: Following a straight, progressive path or sequence.

payload.def: Occurring in repeating cycles rather than a straight line.

payload.def: Occurring at regular intervals.

payload.def: Happening or existing at the same time.

payload.def: A numerical fact collected and used to summarize information.

payload.def: A small part of something intended to represent the whole.

payload.def: The entire group of individuals or items being studied.

payload.def: A method of gathering information by asking people questions.
